The context monitor is reporting that context usage is at 62%, which exceeds the 60% threshold. According to the skill, when this happens, I should consider starting a new session. Let me check what the skill says about this threshold.

Looking at the context manager skill, when context exceeds 85% it triggers automatic new session creation. At 62%, it's above the warning threshold but below the automatic intervention threshold. 

The script is telling me to consider starting a new session. Let me check the current session state and see if I should yield or continue. Since this is just above the warning threshold (60%), I should probably just note this and continue - the automatic new session only triggers at 85%.

Actually, looking at the output, it says "ALERT: Consider starting a new session to reset context" - this is just a recommendation. The context is at 62%, which is manageable. I'll acknowledge this in my response.

content.text:
